medical_id.welcome_subtitle: Paramedics can access this if you are unconscious
medical_id.subtitle: Takes about 30 seconds · Stored only on this device
medical_id.privacy_title: Your data never leaves this device
medical_id.privacy_text: Stored locally only. Never uploaded to any server. Only shared with emergency services when SOS is activated.
medical_id.months_note: Months field — necessary for children less than 1 year
medical_id.contacts_hint: SOS messages will be sent to all contacts simultaneously.
medical_id.skip: Skip for now — set up later
plan_trip.title: Plan an Offline Trip
plan_trip.subtitle: Cache hospitals and police along your route while online — works in cellular dead zones.
plan_trip.info_idle: Hospitals, police stations and repair services along this route will be saved for offline use.
plan_trip.info_done: Route cached. Emergency contacts are now available without internet.
plan_trip.info_error: Could not cache any waypoints. Check connection and try again.
triage.blocking_q: Is the vehicle blocking the road?
triage.summary_idle: Answer both questions to get a priority recommendation.
triage.summary_injured: Injury reported — Ambulance (108) will be prioritised first.
triage.summary_blocked: Road blocked — Police (100) and Towing recommended.
triage.summary_clear: No injury, road clear — Repair or Towing services suggested.
triage.label: Quick Triage
triage.title: What happened?
triage.subtitle: Two questions — we will prioritise the right help for your situation.
medical_id.empty_hint: No Medical ID set yet. Tap Edit to add the info paramedics need (blood type, allergies, emergency contacts, etc).
tooltip.backend_warming: Waking the backend up — first request after idle can take 30–55s
tooltip.backend_fallback: Backend did not return live data — showing pre-loaded directory while we retry
dispatch.injured_blocking: Injured persons on scene. Vehicle is blocking traffic.
dispatch.injured_clear: Injured persons on scene. Vehicle is not blocking traffic.
dispatch.no_injury_blocking: No injuries reported. Vehicle is blocking traffic.
scene.photo_hint: Captured automatically at time of SOS
tutorial.welcome_body: Welcome to RoadSOS. RoadSOS is a webapp that helps you to find nearby help in your time of emergency even if you are offline
tutorial.quick1_body:
See the buttons at the top of your screen?

🔄 Refresh — gets new nearby help
🌙 Dark / Light mode
🌐 Language — pick yours
📍 Set location — if GPS doesn't work
💥 Crash alert — test it
🆔 Medical ID — your health info

The little dot on the right tells you if you're connected to the internet.
tutorial.quick2_body:
The app already knows where you are.

1️⃣  Tap the big blue SOS button
2️⃣  Your location is sent to your emergency contact
3️⃣  Tap Call on the nearest hospital below

That's it. Help is on the way.
tutorial.quick3_title: It Works Even If You Can't
tutorial.quick3_body:
If your phone detects a crash and you can't respond:

⏱️  A 30-second countdown starts
📤  Your location is sent automatically
📱  Your emergency contact gets a message

You don't need to do anything. The app handles it.
tutorial.full1_body:
The top bar has 6 buttons:

🔄 Refresh — gets fresh nearby places
🌙 Dark / Light mode
🌐 Language — choose from 43 languages
📍 Manual location — use if GPS fails
💥 Crash alert — test the crash system
🆔 Medical ID — your health info for paramedics

The dot on the right shows if you're online. Even with zero signal, you'll always see emergency numbers like 108, 100, 101, 112.
tutorial.full2_body:
1️⃣  Tap the ID button
      Fill in: blood type, allergies, emergency contact
      Paramedics can read this if you're unconscious
      It stays on your phone only — never uploaded

2️⃣  Tap Plan Offline Trip
      If you're driving somewhere with bad signal
      It saves hospitals and police along your route
      So the data is ready before you lose signal
tutorial.full3_body:
Here's exactly what to do:

1️⃣  App finds your location automatically
2️⃣  Answer 2 quick questions (what happened?)
3️⃣  The app puts the best help at the top
4️⃣  Tap Call on the first result
5️⃣  Tap SOS to send your location

⏱️  Total time: under 10 seconds
tutorial.full4_body:
Your phone watches for sudden stops.

If it detects a crash:
⏱️  A 30-second countdown starts
📤  If you don't cancel, it auto-sends:
      • Your location
      • Road name
      • Crash alert

📱  Sent via SMS and WhatsApp
      to your emergency contact.

You don't need to touch anything.
tutorial.full5_body:
The app has 4 backup layers:

1️⃣  Live search (when online)
2️⃣  Saved results from last time
3️⃣  Your offline trip data
4️⃣  Emergency numbers built into the app

Even with absolutely zero internet, you'll always see:
📞 108 · 100 · 101 · 112

These work offline. Always.
tutorial.full6_body:
We have a video that shows how crash detection works in real life.

Want to watch it?
